title: celtis laevigata
intro: Celtis laevigata, also known as the Texas hackberry or sugarberry, is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Celtis laevigata include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
